definition: if you assess something, you examine it carefully and say how good or bad it is. If a student’s work is assessed, it is given a grade or mark
example: how is your degree assessed?
the teacher made a quick assessment of the class

definition: an assignment is a piece of work you have to do as part of your job or your studies
example: hand in your assignment by next Friday
many teachers also assign additional reading activities

definition: counselling is help and support you can get from someone who is trained to listen to people describing their problems
example: a counselling course for speech therapists
she always offered us wise counsel
she always offered us wise counsel

definition: coursework is the work a student has to do during a course. The marks for coursework are combined with the marks for exams to provide the student’s final grade
example: did you have much coursework?
translation: учебная работа в течение курса, курсовая работа

definition: your CV is a document which describes your education and work history, and which you show to people when you apply for a job. It is the initials of the Latin curriculum vitae. In American English, they say résumé
example: it will look good on your CV

definition: if someone or something is encouraging, they do or say things that help you believe you can succeed in what you are doing
example: our tutors were very encouraging and gave us lots of feedback
we encourage students to follow their own interests in their research
my friends offered some encouragement

definition: if someone gives you feedback, they tell you how well or badly you did a piece of work, and tell you useful things about how you can improve
example: our tutors were very encouraging and gave us lots of feedback
I’ll feed back to you after I’ve read your other essay

definition: IT stands for Information Technology. IT is the use of computers for storing and distributing information
example: he works in the company’s IT department

definition: leadership is all the qualities and skills that are needed to be a good leader
example: I’m doing a leadership training course
effective schools need effective leaders
effective schools need effective leaders

definition: a module is a single unit of study that is part of a university or college course
example: I failed my last module
the modular course allows for great flexibility

definition: overall means including and considering every aspect of something, not just individual parts of it
example: 25% of the overall mark is from coursework
she finished second overall

definition: progress is improvement that you make over time in something you are studying or trying to achieve
example: I wasn’t making much progress at first
students progress at different speeds

definition: if something is relevant, it has a direct connection to what is happening or what is being talked about
example: the course wasn’t relevant to my current work
pupils need to understand the relevance of politics to their life
collocations: be of relevance to someone or something

example: Andy kept making totally irrelevant remarks
collocations: be of relevance to someone or something

definition: a seminar is a meeting where people discuss a particular subject in a lot of detail. At a university or college, a seminar is a class involving a small group of students and a teacher
example: a one-day seminar on the causes of the first world war

definition: if people are supportive, they understand your situation and help you
example: there were one or two guys who weren’t so supportive
we’ll support you whatever you decide to do
we’ll support you whatever you decide to do

definition: a therapist is someone who provides treatment for people with mental or physical illnesses, but without the use of drugs
example: a speech therapist
I don’t know if her therapy actually helped her
swimming can be highly therapeutic

definition: a tutor is a teacher, especially at a university. A private tutor is a teacher who teaches one student or a small group of students in their homes
example: she has been senior tutor here since 2008
we tutor students of all levels of ability

definition: your workload is the amount of work that you need to do
example: I don’t have time for that on top of my current workload

definition: a workshop is an event where a group of people discuss and learn about a particular topic, especially by doing practical exercises
example: we attended a workshop on language teaching

definition: academic refers to subjects and activity connected to studying at school or university
example: the school has a good academic reputation
a popular and academically gifted student (a very clever student)

definition: someone who is ambitious wants to be very successful and works hard to make sure that they succeed. A project that is ambitious is one that is difficult, but that people work hard to achieve
example: he’s very bright, but not very ambitious
he ambitiously attempted to translate War and Peace within three months
he finally realised his ambition when he became CEO of the company

definition: your approach to a task or problem is the particular way you deal with it
example: they adopted an alternative approach to teaching English
as a teacher, I would approach the issue very differently

definition: attention is the thought or interest you give to something you are listening to or watching
example: she never pays attention in class

definition: to do something on a particular basis means to do it in the way described
example: a lot of kids skip school on a regular basis (regularly)

definition: someone who is bright is very clever and intelligent
example: he’s very bright – always top of his class

definition: a certificate is an official document which states that particular facts are actually true
example: you need to produce a doctor’s certificate within two days
I need a document certifying that I have registered for the course
classes are taught by certified instructors

definition: a detention is a school punishment in which a student has to stay at school for some time after the other students have gone home
example: if they break the rules, give them a detention

definition: discipline is making people follow rules and punishing them if they break the rules
example: the school believes in strict discipline
if you break the rules, you will be disciplined (punished)

definition: someone who is enthusiastic shows their interest in something in a very lively way
example: the audience was wildly enthusiastic
her classmates have enthusiastically welcomed her back
his speech aroused a new enthusiasm among the citizens

definition: fees are the money you have to pay for a particular service
example: my solicitor charges a fee of £150 an hour

definition: if you get into a school or university, you succeed in being accepted there as a student
example: it’s really difficult to get into Oxford or Cambridge

definition: a lecturer is someone who teaches at a college or university
example: he was promoted to senior lecturer last year
in four years there, he delivered over 500 lectures

definition: you use overseas to describe people, places, or things that come from a foreign country
example: we welcome overseas students here
he often travels overseas for work

definition: if you have to produce a document, you have to prove that you have it by showing it to someone in authority
example: you need to produce a doctor’s certificate within two days
you are permitted to drive in Zimbabwe on production of a full UK driving licence

definition: if you push someone to do something, you make them do it by encouraging them very firmly or forcing them to do it
example: they really push pupils to achieve high marks

definition: the reputation of a person, place, or organisation is how good or bad people say it is
example: the school has a good academic reputation

definition: a requirement is something that is needed in order for something else to happen, or for someone to be allowed to do something
example: the minimum requirement to enter the profession is a degree
the law requires employers to provide paid holidays to its staff

definition: respect for someone is a belief that they are important and that they should be treated well and politely
example: if you’re strict from the start, you’ll gain their respect
we were taught to respect our parents
collocations: be respectful of/towards someone or something

example: he was a highly respected journalist
collocations: be respectful of/towards someone or something

example: he was always very respectful towards my parents
collocations: be respectful of/towards someone or something

example: the audience listened respectfully to all the speeches
collocations: be respectful of/towards someone or something

definition: to reverse a decision or rule means to make the opposite decision or to say that the rule is no longer a rule
example: do you think the rules should be reversed?
this policy reversal was completely unexpected

definition: a rough place or district is not very safe because there is a lot of violence or crime there
example: the school is in quite a rough area

definition: if you skip something, you deliberately do not do it or have it
example: a lot of kids skip school at least once a week

definition: a uniform is a set of clothes that is worn by all the people in a particular group, for example by the police, by soldiers in the army, or by pupils in some schools
example: pupils have to wear uniform
drivers must stop when a uniformed police officer asks them to

definition: if you analyse something, you examine it in a lot of detail so that you can understand and explain everything about it
example: I have only just started to analyse the data from the research

definition: an analysis is a detailed examination of something which you do in order to understand and explain everything about it
example: the company conducted a detailed analysis of the situation

definition: if you carry out a particular activity, you do it
example: similar experiments were carried out in France

definition: debate is serious discussion about an important topic or decision
example: there is a debate about how to achieve better results
the most hotly debated subject is Scottish independence

definition: if two things differ, they are not the same as each other. If two people differ, they do not agree about something
example: the effects of the drug differ from person to person
there are different ways we can approach the problem
there’s only a small difference between the two

definition: if something is effective, it succeeds in doing what it is meant to do
example: this approach encourages more effective planning
how do you effectively manage a team?

definition: if something exceeds another thing, it is bigger, better, or more impressive than the other thing
example: your essay should not exceed 750 words
the novel sold in excess of 5 million copies
the novel sold in excess of 5 million copies

definition: an expectation is a situation that you think or hope will happen
example: the film exceeded all our expectations
I didn’t expect to get such a high mark

definition: a failure is someone or something that has not been successful
example: the whole project ended in failure
his attempt to become an MP failed at the election

definition: if something has an impact on a situation, it has a strong effect on it
example: the campaign had little impact on the way people voted
the strike severely impacted the company’s profits

definition: if there is an improvement in something, it has become better
example: there has been a big improvement in your work
my English is really improving

definition: your interpretation of something is how you understand it and what you think it means
example: Hattie’s interpretation is that passionate teachers make a difference
the lack of objection was interpreted as agreement

definition: someone who is passionate is very enthusiastic about what they are doing, and believes in it very strongly
example: passionate teachers make a difference
he passionately believed in the freedom of the individual
his great passion in life is cricket

definition: if something scores, or scores well, it is successful in what it is trying to achieve
example: what factors score best in improving performance

definition: if you specialise in something, you spend most of your working time or studying time on it, and know a lot about it
example: he specialised in economics at university
a specialist in data analysis
